steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both examples follow the same path: an Indigenous language (Nahuatl or Quechua) to Spanish, then Spanish to English. The question asks which claim these examples best support.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Spanish mediated Indigenous influence on English: Both coyote and condor entered English via Spanish, making Spanish the intermediary. ✓
  • Choice B — English borrowed directly from Indigenous languages: The examples show Spanish as an intermediate step, not direct borrowing. ✗
  • Choice C — Indigenous languages borrowed from Spanish: The borrowing runs the opposite direction in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — Nahuatl and Quechua are closely related: No claim about the relationship between these languages is made. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
